zh.lifeSummary: 當John William Webster II於1510年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾可希頓時，他的父親John Webster I25歲，母親Lady Emma Wallis15歲。他於1534年1月10日與Isabel Constance Kythin在英國萊希斯特斯爾可希頓結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1575年6月24日在他的家鄉享年65歲，埋葬在英國萊希斯特斯爾可希頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John William Webster II于1510年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾可希頓时，他的父亲John Webster I25岁，母亲Lady Emma Wallis15岁。他于1534年1月10日与Isabel Constance Kythin在英国萊希斯特斯爾可希頓结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1575年6月24日在他的家乡享年65岁，埋葬在英国萊希斯特斯爾可希頓。

nameMeanings.name: Webster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webbester ‘weaver’ (Old English webbestre ‘female weaver’). By the time of surname formation, the gender distinction of the -stre suffix had almost completely disappeared. Compare Webb , Webber , and Weaver .

History: The name Webster was brought to North America from England independently by several different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One John Webster settled in Ipswich, MA, in 1635; another John Webster (died 1661), ancestor of the lexicographer Noah Webster, emigrated to Cambridge, MA, c. 1631 and later became one of the founders of the colony of CT, of which he was appointed governor in 1656. Daniel Webster (1782–1852), politician and orator, was born in Salisbury, NH, a descendant of Thomas Webster, a prominent 17th-century citizen of Ipswich, MA, whose family had settled there around 1635, while he was still a child.

variants.name: Webb
meanings: English: occupational name for a weaver, from early Middle English webbe (Old English webba (masculine) or webbe (feminine), probably used of both male and female weavers). This word survived into Middle English long enough to give rise to the surname, but was already obsolescent as an agent noun; hence the secondary forms with the agent suffixes -er and -ster (see Webster , Webber and compare Weaver ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, cognates of 1, including Weber and Weberman.
History: Richard Webb, a Lowland Scot, was an admitted freeman of Boston in 1632, and in 1635 was one of the first settlers of Hartford, CT.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When John William Webster II was born in 1510, in Cossington, Leicestershire, England, his father, John Webster I, was 25 and his mother, Lady Emma Wallis, was 15. He married Isabel Constance Kythin on 10 January 1534, in Cossington, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 24 June 1575, in his hometown, at the age of 65, and was buried in Cossington, Leicestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John William Webster II est né en 1510, à Cossington, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, John Webster I, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Emma Wallis, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Isabel Constance Kythin le 10 janvier 1534, à Cossington, Leicestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 24 juin 1575, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Cossington, Leicestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als John William Webster II 1510 in Cossington, Leicestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Webster I 25 und seine Mutter Lady Emma Wallis 15. Er hat am 10. Januar 1534 in Cossington, Leicestershire, England Isabel Constance Kythin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 24. Juni 1575 im Alter von 65 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Cossington, Leicestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John William Webster II è nato il nel 1510 nel Cossington, Leicestershire, England, il suo padre, John Webster I, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Lady Emma Wallis, aveva 15 anni. Lui sposato Isabel Constance Kythin il 10 gennaio 1534, nel Cossington, Leicestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 24 giugno 1575, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Cossington, Leicestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John William Webster IIが1510年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアコシントンで生まれた時，父John Webster Iは25歳，母Lady Emma Wallisは15歳であった。1534年1月10日にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアコシントンでIsabel Constance Kythinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1575年6月24日に故郷で65歳で死去し，イギリスレアイセステルシュアイアコシントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John William Webster II(이)가 1510년 영국 레이스스터실르 코징턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Webster I(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Emma Wallis(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1534년 1월 10일 영국 레이스스터실르 코징턴에서 Isabel Constance Kythin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1575년 6월 24일 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 레이스스터실르 코징턴에 묻혔다.

mn.Leicester, Leicestershire, England, Unit: Leicester, Ratae Coritanorum, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John William Webster II nasceu em 1510, em Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Webster I, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Emma Wallis, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel Constance Kythin em 10 de janeiro de 1534, em Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de junho de 1575, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John William Webster II родился в 1510 г. в местности коссингтон, лисестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Webster I было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Emma Wallis, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Constance Kythin 10 января 1534 г. в местности коссингтон, лисестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 24 июня 1575 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности коссингтон, лисестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John William Webster II nació en 1510, en Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Webster I, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Lady Emma Wallis, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Isabel Constance Kythin el 10 de enero de 1534, en Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 24 de junio de 1575, en su pueblo, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.
